title: Electricity generation from renewables
subtitle: Measured in [terawatt-hours](#dod:watt-hours). Renewable sources include solar, wind, hydropower, bioenergy, geothermal, wave, and tidal.
caption:
Electricity generation from renewables

Measured in terawatt-hours. Renewable sources include solar, wind, hydropower, bioenergy, geothermal, wave, and tidal.

This choropleth map displays global electricity generation from renewable sources in 2025, measured in terawatt-hours. The visualization uses a continuous color scale ranging from pale green for the lowest generation levels to deep navy blue for the highest, with specific thresholds marked at 0, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1,000, and 2,000 TWh. China and the United States occupy the darkest band on the scale, indicating generation exceeding 1,000 TWh, standing as the clear global leaders in volume. Brazil and Canada appear in a medium-dark blue band, placing them in the 200 to 500 TWh range, significantly ahead of most other nations but below the top two. India, Russia, and Norway sit in the lighter blue band corresponding to 100 to 200 TWh. A broad cluster of countries including Australia, Mexico, Spain, and Turkey falls within the 50 to 100 TWh range, shown in a distinct teal shade. Much of Western Europe, including Germany, France, and the United Kingdom, along with Japan, occupies the 20 to 50 TWh band. Large portions of the Global South, particularly across central Africa and parts of South America outside Brazil, appear in the palest green shades, indicating generation between 0 and 10 TWh. Greenland, Western Sahara, and several island nations are marked with diagonal hatching to denote no available data. The distribution shows a heavy concentration of renewable electricity generation in a small number of large economies, while the majority of countries remain in the lower tiers of the scale.
